text: The phrenotomy was started from the anterior mediastinum and then extended posteriorly.
The transdiphragmatic approach was first performed through the four ports with low-pressure capnothorax of 4 or 5 mm.
The peritoneal fat was exposed with the 3 mm traction instrument.
Meticulous division of the venous and arterial drainage ligation was performed with CLIP.
An articulated bipolar device was used for inferior gland dissection.
The specimen was extracted and blocked through a 10 mm incision.
Hemostasis was completed with insertion of an hemostatic sealant in the surgical bed and the diaphragm was closed using uninterrupted stitches.
Further, a VATS S1, S2 and S3 segmentectomy was performed with ELOR and mediastinal lymphadenectomy.
